skill.name: longbridge-earnings
skill.description: Analyze pre-earnings and post-earnings events with Longbridge market data, consensus estimates, filings, news, valuation methods, and Markdown reporting workflows.
skill.summary: Create earnings previews, summary cards, and full earnings reports using Longbridge data.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, filesystem, network, and sensitive-data patterns. AI review found most Markdown hits are false positives, but confirmed that scripts/collect.py runs the Longbridge CLI, performs network-backed data collection, and writes raw outputs to a temp directory. No malicious intent or prompt-injection attempt was found, so publication is acceptable with a medium-risk warning.

medium_findings.title: External Longbridge CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The collector runs the longbridge executable with a user-provided symbol and many fixed subcommands. It uses subprocess.run with an argument list and no shell, which reduces injection risk, but it still executes an external credentialed CLI and depends on that CLI behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly invokes subprocess.run for the Longbridge CLI. The symbol is passed as an argv element rather than through a shell string, so command injection evidence is not present.

medium_findings.title: Network-Backed Market Data Collection
medium_findings.description: The workflow instructs the assistant to collect Longbridge data, use MCP as a fallback, and perform limited web searches for transcripts or consensus context. This is expected for an earnings research skill, but users should understand that ticker requests and research context may leave the local session.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files explicitly direct use of the Longbridge CLI, MCP endpoint, and web search. This is legitimate functionality, but it is still a network disclosure surface.

medium_findings.title: Raw Data Stored in Temporary Directory
medium_findings.description: The collector writes raw JSON and error files to a temp directory and prints the RAW_DIR path for reuse. This supports full-report workflows, but temporary files may retain market data, filings, errors, or account-scoped CLI output longer than users expect.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file writes and RAW_DIR disclosure are explicit. I did not find evidence that credentials are written, so the concern is local data retention rather than confirmed secret leakage.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Command Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Most Ruby or shell backtick detections occur inside Markdown examples, command names, tables, and financial formulas. They document commands or output formats rather than executing code inside the skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is fenced or inline Markdown documentation. No evidence found that these Markdown backticks execute as Ruby or shell code.

low_findings.title: Weak-Crypto and SAM Detections Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm detections match ordinary words in the skill name and earnings instructions, not cryptographic code. The Windows SAM database detection appears to match the word SAME in a report checklist sentence.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain descriptive earnings text and the phrase SAME quarter. No evidence found of hashing, password storage, or Windows SAM access.

low_findings.title: Sibling Skill Links Are Not Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The path traversal detections are relative Markdown links to sibling Longbridge skills. They do not instruct the assistant or script to read arbitrary parent directories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged ../ sequences appear only in a related-skills table. No evidence found that scripts use these paths for filesystem access.

dangerous_patterns.title: subprocess.run Invokes External CLI
dangerous_patterns.description: scripts/collect.py executes the longbridge CLI through subprocess.run. The argument-list form avoids shell expansion, but marketplace users should review the external CLI trust boundary.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess call is direct and confirmed. The absence of shell=True lowers exploitability but does not remove external-command risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Persistent Temporary Raw Output
dangerous_patterns.description: The script creates a reusable temp directory for raw JSON and error files. This may expose collected research data to other local processes depending on host permissions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The temp directory is explicitly created and printed. I did not find evidence of credential collection, so impact is bounded to retained output data.

content.value_statement: Earnings analysis requires fast collection of filings, estimates, prices, and management commentary. This skill turns Longbridge data into previews, summary cards, and full Markdown reports.
actual_capabilities: Collects Longbridge snapshot, statement, consensus, forecast, quote, rating, segment, news, and kline data.
Creates a default post-earnings summary card with beat or miss context and key operating trends.
Produces full Markdown earnings reports when users request deeper research coverage.
Builds pre-earnings previews from guidance, recent events, prior calls, and consensus gaps.
Applies DCF, trading comparable, and precedent transaction frameworks for valuation sections.
Supports US, Hong Kong, and A-share ticker formats when Longbridge data is available.
limitations: Requires the Longbridge CLI or configured Longbridge MCP access for primary data collection.
Financial output depends on available Longbridge data, filings, transcripts, and search results.
It does not replace professional investment, legal, tax, or accounting advice.
Full reports can require web searches for transcripts, consensus history, or transaction data.

use_cases.title: Prepare for an Upcoming Earnings Release
use_cases.description: Review prior guidance, recent events, consensus gaps, and watch items before a company reports results.

use_cases.title: Summarize Fresh Quarterly Results
use_cases.description: Create a concise earnings card covering KPI variance, segments, margins, Street view, and near-term risks.

use_cases.title: Draft a Full Earnings Update Report
use_cases.description: Build a structured Markdown report with valuation math, estimate revisions, thesis updates, and source notes.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Earnings Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the latest earnings for TSLA.US and give me the default summary card.

prompt_templates.title: Pre-Earnings Preview
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a pre-earnings preview for 700.HK. Focus on prior guidance, recent events, and key questions to watch.

prompt_templates.title: Full Markdown Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a full earnings report for NVDA.US with valuation, estimate revisions, risks, and source references.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Thesis Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze 9988.HK after earnings. Compare reported results with consensus, update the thesis pillars, and explain valuation sensitivity.

output_examples.input: Analyze the latest earnings for AAPL.US.
output_examples.output: A concise earnings card with headline verdict, core KPI table, segment revenue mix, margin trend, Street view, next-quarter consensus, and risk tags.

output_examples.input: Prepare a full earnings report for 700.HK.
output_examples.output: A Markdown research report covering results, segments, profitability, guidance, earnings call themes, estimate changes, valuation, thesis update, risks, and sources.

output_examples.input: What should I watch before MSFT.US reports?
output_examples.output: A pre-earnings preview that reviews prior management guidance, recent company events, consensus gaps, historical guidance accuracy, and key watch items.

best_practices: Confirm the ticker, market suffix, and reporting period before relying on the output.
Use the default summary card first, then request a full report only when deeper valuation work is needed.
Treat all valuation and rating language as research support, not as personal financial advice.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill when Longbridge data access is unavailable and no public sources can confirm the quarter.
Do not ask it to fabricate missing estimates, transcript quotes, or management guidance.
Do not rely on a full report without reviewing assumptions, sources, and valuation sensitivity.

faq.question: Does this skill need Longbridge access?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed around the Longbridge CLI, with MCP and web search as fallback sources.

faq.question: Can it analyze both pre-earnings and post-earnings situations?
faq.answer: Yes. It has separate workflows for upcoming earnings previews and reported quarterly results.

faq.question: What markets does it support?
faq.answer: The instructions target US, Hong Kong, and A-share symbols when the Longbridge data source supports them.

faq.question: Can it create a file output?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create a Markdown report when the user explicitly asks for a full report or preview file.

faq.question: Does it provide investment advice?
faq.answer: No. It organizes financial data and valuation assumptions for research. Users must make their own decisions.

faq.question: Why is the security rating medium?
faq.answer: The skill runs an external CLI, uses network-backed data sources, and stores raw outputs in a temp directory.
